it:46.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
it:46.3: "Monks, live with the trainings [in heightened virtue, heightened mind, and heightened discernment] as your reward, with discernment uppermost, release the essence, and mindfulness the governing principle. As you live with the trainings as your reward, with discernment uppermost, release the essence, and mindfulness the governing principle, then one of two fruits can be expected: either gnosis right in the present life, or — if there be any remnant of clinging-sustenance — non-return."
it:46.4: Complete in the training, not subject to falling away, one with discernment uppermost, seeing the stopping, the ending of birth: that sage bears his last body, has shaken off Māra, I tell you, has gone beyond aging.
it:46.5: So, always delighting in jhana, centered, ardent, seeing the stopping, the ending of birth, conquering Māra, along with his host, monks, be gone-beyond aging and death.
it:46.6: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
